data.title: China Shenhua Energy Company Limited Reports Sales Results for the Month Ended January 2026
data.description:
China Shenhua Energy Company Limited reported sales results for the month ended January 2026. For the month, the company reported Coal sales of 33.2 million tonnes against 30.2 million tonnes a year ago. Polyethylene sales were 31,000 tonnes compared to 31,800 tonnes a year ago.

Polypropylene sales were 29,000 tonnes compared to 29,800 tonnes a year ago.

data.title: China Shenhua Energy Company Limited Reports Production Results for the Month Ended January 2026
data.description: China Shenhua Energy Company Limited reported production results for the month ended January 2026. For the month, the company reported Commercial coal production of 27.4 million tonnes against 24.9 million tonnes a year ago. Gross power generation was 22.22 billion kWh against 16.56 billion kWh a year ago.

China Southern Airlines Company Limited Reports Operating Results for the Month Ended January 2026
data.description:
China Southern Airlines Company Limited reported operating results for the month ended January 2026. For the month, the company reported RPK of 28,103.01 million. RTK was 3,315.77 million.

RTK - Cargo and Mail was 856.49 million. ASK was 33,755.01 million. ATK was 4,737.62 million.

Passengers carried was 13,988,620. Cargo and mail carried was 160,020 tonnes. ATK - Cargo and Mail was 1,699.67 million.

Passenger Load Factor was 83.26%. Cargo and Mail Load Factor was 50.39%. Overall Load Factor was 69.99%.

data.title: Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. Announces Acceptance of Subsidiary's Drug Registration Application
data.description:
Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical (Group) Co., Ltd. announced that recently, the drug registration application of Moxetomidate Hydrochloride Injection (project code: ET-26; registration category of application: class 1 chemical medicine; hereinafter the "New Drug") of Jinzhou Avanc Pharmaceutical Co., Ltd. (Jin Zhou Ao Hong Yao Ye You Xian Ze Ren Gong Si), a subsidiary of Shanghai Fosun Pharmaceutical (Group) Co., Ltd. (Shang Hai Fu Xing Yi Yao (Ji Tuan)Gu Fen You Xian Gong Si) (the "Company"), was accepted by the National Medical Products Administration. The intended indication under this application is for the induction and maintenance of anesthesia during short surgical procedures. The New Drug is subject to, among others, the passing of the GMP compliance inspection and the drug registration approval before commercial production.

This acceptance of the drug registration application will not have a material impact on the results of the Group at this stage. Due to the industry characteristics of the pharmaceutical products, the specific sales performance after the market launch of pharmaceutical products may be affected by factors including, but not limited to, the demand for medication, market competition and sales channels, etc., and is subject to considerable uncertainty. Investors should take note of the investment risks.

Announcement is hereby made.

data.title: China Eastern Airlines Corporation Limited Reports Unaudited Operating Results for the Month Ended January 2026
data.description:
China Eastern Airlines Corporation Limited reported unaudited operating results for the month ended January 2026. For the month, the company reported Passenger Transportation Data ASK (available-seat-kilometres) of 26,845.73 million against 27,830.73 million a year ago. RPK (revenue passenger-kilometres) was 22,820.29 million against 23,057.14 million a year ago.

Number of passengers carried was 12.20057 million against 12.68352 million a year ago. Passenger load factor was 85.01% against 82.85% a year ago. Freight Transportation Data AFTK (available freight tonne-kilometres) was 917.80 million against 978.17 million a year ago.

RFTK (Revenue freight tonne-kilometres) was 363.49 million against 335.54 million a year ago. Weight of freight carried was 98.76 million kg against 97.48 million kg a year ago. Freight load factor was 39.60% against 34.30% a year ago.

Consolidated ATK (available tonne kilometers) was 3,333.92 million against 3,482.94 million a year ago. Consolidated RTK (revenue tonne kilometers) was 2,371.39 million against 2,332.64 million a year ago. Consolidated overall load factor was 71.13% against 66.97% a year ago.

data.title: Air China Limited Reports Group Operating Results for the Month of January 2026
data.description: Air China Limited reported group operating results for the month of January 2026. For the month, the company's ATK was 3,963.8 million, ASK was 31,101.3 million, AFTK was 1,160.0 million, Traffic RTK was 2,665.4 million, Traffic RPK was 25,567.9 million, Traffic RFTK was 412.6 million, Number of Passengers carried were 13,275.6, Total Cargo and Mail carried were 125,979.6 tonnes, Passenger Load Factor was 82.2%, Cargo and Mail Load Factor was 35.6% and Overall Load Factor was 67.2%.

data.title: Meituan Provides Earning Guidance for the Year Ended December 31, 2025
data.description:
Meituan provided earning guidance for the year ended December 31, 2025. For the period, the Group expects to record a loss in the range of approximately RMB 23,300 million to RMB 24,300 million for the year ended December 31, 2025 (the "FY2025"), as compared to a profit of approximately RMB 35,808 million for the year ended December 31, 2024 (the "FY2024"). The expected loss of the Group in FY2025 was primarily due to a reversal from an operating profit of approximately RMB 52,415 million in FY2024 to an operating loss of approximately RMB 6,800 million to RMB 7,000 million in FY2025 for the Core Local Commerce segment, together with increased investments in the Group's overseas businesses.

In particular, in response to the unprecedentedly intense industry competition in FY2025, the Group strategically increased investments across its entire ecosystem to strengthen its core advantages and drive sustainable growth. These initiatives mainly included: (i) on the consumer front, enhancing marketing and promotional efforts to strengthen brand awareness and price competitiveness, thereby continuously improving user transaction activity and engagement; (ii) on the delivery front, increasing incentives and enriching benefits for couriers to ensure service quality and enhance user experience; and (iii) on the merchant front, maintaining proactive resource investment to support merchants in improving operational efficiency, expanding consumer reach, iterating business models, and achieving steady growth.

data.title: U.S. withdraws Chinese military backed companies list-Federal Register
data.description:
WASHINGTON, Feb 13 (Reuters) - The Trump administration on Friday withdrew an updated version of its list of Chinese military-backed companies that was posted earlier on Friday and included Chinese tech giants Alibaba and Baidu. 

It was not immediately clear whether the withdrawal indicated a change of policy. The Pentagon did not immediately respond to a request for comment. 

(Reporting by Alexandra Alper and Mike Stone; Editing by Chizu Nomiyama )

data.title: The City of Atlanta Modernizes Constituent Services and Unlocks AI-Driven Innovation with Oracle
data.description:
The City of Atlanta is expanding its relationship with Oracle and Deloitte by selecting Oracle Permitting and Licensing (OPAL) to modernize city planning, zoning, permitting, code enforcement, and inspections. This expanded collaboration, building on the City of Atlanta's previous adoption of Oracle Fusion Cloud Applications for finance and HR, will help streamline citizen services, reduce processing times, and support Atlanta's economic growth. As Georgia's capital and largest city, Atlanta is experiencing rapid growth, with its population projected to increase by 30% by 2050.

To meet rising demand and address the limitations of legacy permitting and licensing systems, the City of Atlanta sought a modern, user-friendly solution to automate key workflows and make services more accessible for residents and businesses. Building on the rollout of Oracle Fusion Applications for finance and HR, the City of Atlanta has again chosen Oracle and Deloitte to provide an effective, unified IT experience across municipal operations. By implementing Oracle Permitting and Licensing, the City of Atlanta will unify agency operations within a single, integrated system; enhance transparency and tracking for staff, businesses, and residents; automate reviews and workflows; and minimize approval bottlenecks.

With Oracle Fusion Cloud Enterprise Resource Planning (ERP), part of Oracle Fusion Applications, the City of Atlanta reports that it has already been able to increase productivity, reduce costs, improve financial controls, and expects to save $17.5 million over a 10-year period. In addition, Oracle Fusion Cloud Enterprise Performance Management (EPM) helps the City of Atlanta simplify financial reporting and planning, and accelerate decision-making, while Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management (HCM) enables more detailed workforce insights, streamlined HR processes, and improved employee experience. The City of Atlanta will also leverage Oracle AI Agent Studio to create and manage custom AI agents that complement the AI agents embedded in Oracle Fusion Applications workflows and address city-specific needs.

Oracle Fusion Cloud Human Capital Management ("HCM") Provides a unified AI-powered HR platform that connects all people-related processes and data to help organizations automate tasks throughout the employee lifecycle, improve the employee experience, and give HR leaders actionable workforce insights. Oracle Fusion Cloud Supply Chain & Manufacturing (SCM): Provides a unified AI-powered platform that integrates supply chain and operations processes and helps organizations enhance resilience and quickly adapt to market changes. Oracle Fusion Cloud Customer Experience (CX): Provides a suite of AI-powered applications that helps organizations manage marketing, sales, and service processes to win business, build stronger customer relationships, and improve customer experiences.

Kuala Lumpur Kepong Berhad Reports Production Results for the Month of January 2026
data.description: Kuala Lumpur Kepong Berhad reported production results for the month of January 2026. For the month, the company reported production of fresh fruit bunches of 503,866 mt, Crude Palm Oil of 126,943 mt, Palm Kernel of 24,689 mt and Rubber was 274,714 kg.

data.title: Regrow Israel Selects Oracle Agriculture Data Intelligence to Help Restore and Strengthen Farming Across Country
data.description: ReGrow Israel has chosen Oracle Agriculture Data Intelligence to help restore and strengthen farming across the country. The platform integrates satellite imagery, ground data, and sensor feeds in one environment, giving ReGrow a real-time view into soil conditions, crop progress, and water use to support better, more predictable production. With powerful AI models designed for the acute challenges facing the industry, Oracle Agriculture Intelligence can forecast crop yield, detect potential threats, model impact of proposed intervention programs, and quantify risks to provide organizations and governments with the data needed to make more strategic agriculture planning and resource allocation decisions.

data.title: Sands China Ltd. Appoints Patrick Dumont as Chairman
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands Corp. announced Mr. Patrick Dumont was appointed chairman of Sands China Ltd. Mr. Dumont was appointed president and chief operating officer of Sands in January 2021 and has been a member of the board of directors since 2017. He joined the company in 2010 and has focused on creating long-term growth for the business and its stakeholders in roles with responsibility for operations, properties and capital allocation strategy.

Mr. Dumont has more than 25 years of experience in management, development, operations and corporate finance. Under his leadership, Sands has undertaken transformative investment programs in Macao and Singapore to elevate its product offerings, enhance customer experience and increase long-term earnings potential. He is responsible for the company's new development in Singapore, an $8 billion ultra-luxury project that broke ground in July 2025.

Mr. Dumont received an MBA from Columbia Business School and a Bachelor of Science in mechanical engineering from Johns Hopkins University. Mr. Dumont resides in Las Vegas, Nevada, with his wife and children.

Las Vegas Sands Corp. Announces CEO Changes
data.description:
Las Vegas Sands announced on February 13, 2026 that Patrick Dumont will become the company's chief executive officer effective March 1, 2026. Mr. Dumont will succeed Robert G. Goldstein, who becomes a senior advisor to the company through March 2028. Sands' board of directors had previously indicated Mr. Dumont would be the likely successor to Mr. Goldstein. Mr. Dumont was also appointed chairman of Sands China Ltd., the company's Hong Kong-listed subsidiary.

Mr. Dumont was appointed president and chief operating officer of Sands in January 2021 and has been a member of the board of directors since 2017. He joined the company in 2010 and has focused on creating long-term growth for the business and its stakeholders in roles with responsibility for operations, properties and capital allocation strategy. Mr. Dumont has more than 25 years of experience in management, development, operations and corporate finance.

Under his leadership, Sands has undertaken transformative investment programs in Macao and Singapore to elevate its product offerings, enhance customer experience and increase long-term earnings potential. He is responsible for the company's new development in Singapore, an $8 billion ultra-luxury project that broke ground in July 2025. Mr. Dumont received an MBA from Columbia Business School and a Bachelor of Science in mechanical engineering from Johns Hopkins University.

data.title: FedEx Corp. Declares Quarterly Cash Dividend, Payable on April 1, 2026
data.description:
The Board of Directors of FedEx Corp. declared a quarterly cash dividend of $1.45 per share on FedEx Corp. common stock, in line with the company?s continued focus on delivering stockholder value.

The dividend is payable April 1, 2026, to stockholders of record at the close of business on March 9, 2026.

data.title: US allows oil majors to resume Venezuela operations, broadly okays new energy investments
data.description:
WASHINGTON, Feb 13 (Reuters) - The U.S. eased sanctions on Venezuela's energy sector on Friday, issuing two general licenses that allow global energy companies to resume oil and gas operations in the OPEC member and for other companies to negotiate contracts on investments in new energy operations.

The Treasury Department's Office of Foreign Assets Control issued a general license allowing Chevron, BP, Eni, Shell and Repsol to resume oil and gas operations in Venezuela. 

The other license allows companies around the world to enter contracts for new investments in Venezuelan oil and gas. It does not allow transactions with companies in Russia, Iran, or China or entities owned or controlled by joint ventures with people in those countries.

The move was the biggest relaxation of sanctions on Venezuela since U.S. forces captured and removed President Nicolas Maduro last month. 

The U.S. has had sanctions on Venezuela since 2019 when President Donald Trump imposed them during first his administration.

Trump is now seeking $100 billion in investments by energy companies in Venezuela's energy business. U.S. Energy Secretary Chris Wright said on Thursday, during his second day of a trip to Venezuela, that oil sales from the country since Maduro's capture have hit $1 billion and would hit another $5 billion in months.

Wright said the U.S. will control the proceeds from the sales until Venezuela stands up a "representative government."

Since last month, the Treasury issued several other general licenses to facilitate oil exports, storage, imports and sales from Venezuela. It also authorized the provision of U.S. goods, technology, software or services for the exploration, development or production of oil and gas in Venezuela.

The Venezuelan government seized assets of Exxon Mobil and ConocoPhillips in 2007 under then-President Hugo Chavez. The Trump administration is trying to get those companies to invest in Venezuela as well. At a meeting at the White House with Trump last month, Exxon Mobil CEO Darren Woods said Venezuela was "uninvestable" at the moment.

Wright said on Thursday that Exxon is in talks with the Venezuelan government and gathering data about the oil sector, and Exxon did not immediately comment.   

(Reporting by Timothy Gardner; Editing by Nia Williams)

By Timothy Gardner

OpenAI says China's DeepSeek trained its AI by distilling US models: memo
data.description:
STORY: OpenAI has warned U.S. lawmakers that Chinese AI startup DeepSeek is targeting the ChatGPT maker and the nation's leading AI companies.

To replicate models and use them for its own training.

That's according to a memo seen by Reuters.

Sam Altman-led OpenAI accused DeepSeek of, quote, "ongoing efforts to free-ride on the capabilities developed by OpenAI and other U.S. frontier labs."

The technique is known as distillation.

It involves having an older, more established and powerful AI model evaluate the quality of the answers coming out of a newer model.

And effectively transfers the older model's learnings.

The memo was sent to the U.S. House Select Committee on Strategic Competition between the U.S. and the Chinese Communist Party on Thursday.

DeepSeek and its parent company High-Flyer did not immediately respond to Reuters' requests for comment.

China's DeepSeek shook markets early last year with a set of AI models that rivaled some of the best offerings from the U.S.,.

It fuelled concerns in Washington that China could catch up in the AI race despite restrictions.
